query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "openrath: session-centered runtime state for agent systems" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (d) by providing a foundational framework ("Layered Mutability") and empirical evidence (the "ratchet experiment" measuring identity hysteresis) for the specific mechanism of runtime state persistence and behavioral drift in self-modifying LLM agents, which is the core subject of the user's "OpenRath" follow-up. It directly addresses the "runtime state" and "agent systems" variables by formalizing how mutable internal conditions influence future actions, off
